protocol: Each profile generated a fresh DEFLATE level 6 source workbook in a separate process, ran two warm-ups, retained five measured editor-process runs, and selected the elapsed-time median representative. Only the targeted upsert scenario was measured.
peak_memory_metric: Windows PROCESS_MEMORY_COUNTERS.PeakWorkingSetSize for each benchmark process; source fixture generation is outside measured reused-source runs
transform_metrics: Single-pass transform microseconds plus event-fragment append/flush counts, peak owned batching buffer, relationship scan time, temporary-file write time and a derived source-scan/action residual
package_writer_metrics: Caller-owned internal telemetry enabled only for benchmark save: package and target worksheet entry open/read/write/close timing, bytes and call counts. Normal callers do not collect per-call clock data.
compatibility_protocol: The runner validated output-plan byte accounting and exact compressed payload equality for every raw-copy entry in every warm-up and measured output. The median representative from each profile opened with openpyxl read-only mode.
scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one-million-cell and five-million-cell numeric source worksheets, 1,000 targeted upserts, and output DEFLATE levels 1/6. No arbitrary-workbook, random-editing, cross-machine or Office compatibility claim.
